description.s: Time-based reminders on items, with native OS notifications on Android (Tauri) and desktop.
Architecture

Scheduling logic lives in userland items. A thin native layer translates reminder timestamps into OS-level scheduling calls. The native layer is a dumb translator, not a scheduler — all the "brains" (recurrence rules, snooze, relative times) stay in items.
Items are the source of truth
— reminder time, recurrence, and display text are fields on items (or a dedicated reminder type)
Native layer does two things
— (1) schedule a wake-up alarm at a timestamp via AlarmManager, (2) show a notification via Android's NotificationManager
Boot-scan for durability
— on app startup, scan all items with future reminder times and re-register alarms with the OS. This handles device reboots (which clear Android alarms) and app updates.
Why Not Userland-Only Scheduling?
The obvious Hobson-native approach would be: run a timer in userland, send "notify now" when it fires. This works on desktop (JS timers fire normally). On Android it fails because:
WebView JS is paused in background
— setTimeout/setInterval are throttled or stopped entirely when the Activity is backgrounded. Reminders simply won't fire.
Foreground service doesn't help

— a foreground service keeps the process alive, but the WebView is still paused. You'd need the Rust side to run timers and wake the WebView, which is more work than using AlarmManager and less reliable.
OEM battery optimization
— Xiaomi, Samsung, Huawei etc. aggressively kill background apps. Even foreground services are targets. Native AlarmManager has the best chance of surviving this.
Android Gotchas
POST_NOTIFICATIONS permission
— required on Android 13+. Must handle denial gracefully.
SCHEDULE_EXACT_ALARM permission
— required on Android 12+ for exact alarms. Inexact alarms can be delayed by minutes or hours. For reminders, exact alarms are needed.
Alarms cleared on reboot
— need a BOOT_COMPLETED receiver to re-register, or rely on boot-scan when the user next opens the app.
Tauri notification plugin
— @tauri-apps/plugin-notification supports scheduling in 2.x. Check whether it handles boot persistence natively or if a custom Rust/Kotlin plugin is needed.
Implementation Sketch
Add a "reminder" field (timestamp + optional recurrence rule) to the item schema, or create a dedicated reminder type
Build a date/time picker field view for setting reminders
On save (when reminder field changes): invoke Tauri command to schedule/cancel the native alarm
On boot: scan items for active reminders, re-register with OS
When alarm fires: Tauri Rust side shows notification, optionally wakes the WebView to run any Hob-defined notification logic
Desktop: simpler — JS timers work, just need notification.show(). Can use the same boot-scan approach for consistency.
Priority
Low — useful for personal productivity but requires Tauri-native plugin work (Rust/Kotlin). The userland scheduling logic is straightforward; the hard part is the Android integration.
